mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack triggers earnings recession fears, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative mean 60-day forward return over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, industrial production growth decelerates as capacity constraints bind, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration during late-cycle phases produces positive 5-day forward returns in 70% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 5.0%, high borrowing costs compress corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS > 5.0% periods are statistically indistinguishable from zero with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment signals a tightening labor market that boosts corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline shows a negative or zero Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household wealth and construction sector activity, which mechanically lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month changes exhibit a negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rapid industrial production rebounds signal supply chain normalization and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels above 101.0 during recovery regimes fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases.
